Test Case Name: Valid HR admin login
Preconditions: Application is open; an HR employee record exists in the DB
Test Steps: 1. Enter the HR employee email. 2. Enter the last 4 digits of their SSN as the password. 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: HR menu is displayed and the session is started as HR admin.

Test Case Name: Valid non-HR employee login
Preconditions: Application is open; a non-HR employee record exists in the DB
Test Steps: 1. Enter the employee's email. 2. Enter the last 4 digits of their SSN as the password. 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: Employee menu is displayed (not the HR menu).

Test Case Name: Login with a non-existent email
Preconditions: Application is open
Test Steps: 1. Enter any email that does not exist in the database. 2. Enter any 4-digit password. 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown with message: "Invalid login. Use the employee email and the last 4 digits of the SSN." Password field is cleared.

Test Case Name: Login with the wrong SSN digits
Preconditions: Application is open; a valid employee email is known
Test Steps: 1. Enter a valid employee email. 2. Enter the wrong 4 SSN digits (e.g. 0000 if the real value is different). 3. Click Login.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown with message: "Invalid login." Password field is cleared.

Test Case Name: Login with empty email and password
Preconditions: Application is open
Test Steps: 1. Leave both the email and password fields empty. 2. Click Login.
Expected Result: Error dialog is shown. The system does not navigate away from the login screen.

Test Case Name: Search by valid Employee ID
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Select "Employee ID" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter a known valid employee ID. 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: One row appears in the results table with the correct employee name and details. Status label reads "Found 1 result(s)."

Test Case Name: Search by name (partial match)
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Select "Name" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter a partial first or last name that exists in the DB. 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: All employees with a matching first or last name appear in the table. Status label shows the correct result count.

Test Case Name: Search by DOB with valid format
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Select "DOB" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter a date in YYYY-MM-DD format that matches at least one employee. 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: Matching employees appear in the results table.

Test Case Name: Search by DOB with invalid date format
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Select "DOB" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter a date in the wrong format (e.g. 25/12/1990). 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown: "Use YYYY-MM-DD for DOB searches." Results table stays empty.

Test Case Name: Search by Employee ID with non-numeric value
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Select "Employee ID" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter letters instead of a number (e.g. "abc"). 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown: "Employee ID must be a whole number." Results table stays empty.

Test Case Name: Search with empty field
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Leave the Search Value field empty. 2. Click Search.
Expected Result: Prompt dialog shown: ""Enter a value to search."" Table stays empty.

Test Case Name: Employee searches their own record
Preconditions: Logged in as a regular (non-HR) employee
Test Steps: 1. Select "Employee ID" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter the currently logged-in employee's own ID. 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: The employee's own record appears in the table.

Test Case Name: Employee attempts to view another employee's record
Preconditions: Logged in as a regular (non-HR) employee
Test Steps: 1. Select "Employee ID" in the Search Type dropdown. 2. Enter a different employee's ID. 3. Click Search.
Expected Result: Access-denied warning shown: "Access denied. Employees may only view their own records." Table stays empty.

Test Case Name: Add a new employee with all valid data
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; the address ID being used already exists in the addresses table
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Add Employee. 2. Fill in all fields with valid data (YYYY-MM-DD date; numeric salary / address ID / division ID). 3. Click Add Employee.
Expected Result: Success dialog shows the new employee's empID. Form is cleared. Employee can be found via search.

Test Case Name: Add employee with non-numeric salary
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Add Employee. 2. Fill in all fields but enter letters in the Salary field. 3. Click Add Employee.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown: "Salary, address ID, and division ID must use valid numbers." Form retains all entered values.

Test Case Name: Add employee with invalid hire-date format
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Add Employee. 2. Fill in all fields but enter the date as DD-MM-YYYY (e.g. 15-06-2024). 3. Click Add Employee.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown: "Hire date must use YYYY-MM-DD format." Form retains all entered values.

Test Case Name: Clear form button resets all fields
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Add Employee. 2. Fill in several fields. 3. Click Clear.
Expected Result: All eight input fields are empty.

Test Case Name: HR admin edits and saves an employee record
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; employee record exists
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Select the record and click Edit Selected. 3. Change a field (e.g. email). 4. Click Save.
Expected Result: Success dialog is shown. The changed field is now reflected if the employee is searched again.

Test Case Name: HR admin deletes an employee (confirms deletion)
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; deletable employee record exists
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Select the record and click Delete Selected. 3. In the confirmation dialog click Yes.
Expected Result: Confirmation dialog appears; after clicking Yes the employee is removed from the table and from the database.

Test Case Name: HR admin cancels employee deletion
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; employee record exists
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Select the record and click Delete Selected. 3. In the confirmation dialog click No.
Expected Result: The employee is NOT deleted and remains in the table.

Test Case Name: Bulk update with valid range and positive percentage
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; employees exist within the salary range
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Bulk Salary Update. 2. Enter a min salary, max salary, and a positive percentage (e.g. 5). 3. Click Apply Update. 4. Confirm the update.
Expected Result: Confirmation dialog is shown. After confirming, the result area reads "Updated N employee record(s)" where N > 0.

Test Case Name: Apply update with a negative percentage
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Bulk Salary Update. 2. Enter valid min and max salaries. 3. Enter -10 as the percentage. 4. Click Apply Update.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown: "Percentage increase must be positive." Result area is unchanged.

Test Case Name: Apply update with non-numeric input
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Bulk Salary Update. 2. Enter "abc" in the Min Salary field. 3. Enter valid values in the other fields. 4. Click Apply Update.
Expected Result: Error dialog shown: "Minimum salary, maximum salary, and percentage must use valid numbers." Result area is unchanged.

Test Case Name: Update range with no employees in it
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Bulk Salary Update. 2. Enter a salary range that no employee falls within (e.g. 9999999 to 99999999). 3. Enter 5 as the percentage. 4. Click Apply Update and confirm.
Expected Result: Result area reads "Updated 0 employee record(s)."

Test Case Name: Employee views their own payroll history
Preconditions: Logged in as a regular (non-HR) employee who has payroll records
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Pay History from the employee menu.
Expected Result: All payroll records for the logged-in employee are shown in the table with correct pay date and earnings.

Test Case Name: HR admin views payroll for any employee
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; target employee has payroll records
Test Steps: 1. Search for an employee. 2. Select the record. 3. Click View Pay History.
Expected Result: The payroll table is populated with the selected employee's records showing Pay ID, date, and earnings.

Test Case Name: Run Pay by Job Title report
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; payroll data exists for the chosen month/year
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Reports > Pay by Job Title. 2. Enter a valid month (1-12) and year. 3. Click Run Report.
Expected Result: Table is populated with two columns: Job Title and Total Pay. Each row represents a distinct job title.

Test Case Name: Run Pay by Division report
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; payroll data exists for the chosen month/year
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Reports > Pay by Division. 2. Enter a valid month (1-12) and year. 3. Click Run Report.
Expected Result: Table is populated with two columns: Division and Total Pay. Each row represents a distinct division.

Test Case Name: Run New Hires by Date Range report
Preconditions: Logged in as HR admin; employees were hired within the chosen date range
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to Reports > New Hires. 2. Enter a start date and end date in YYYY-MM-DD format. 3. Click Run Report.
Expected Result: Table is populated with employees whose hire date falls within the specified range.

Test Case Name: Logout clears session and returns to login screen
Preconditions: Any user is logged in
Test Steps: 1. Click Logout (or navigate to the logout option). 2. Observe the screen that is shown.
Expected Result: Login screen is displayed. A subsequent search or navigation attempt does not show any previous session's data.
